Bodytext.67108945: "" "'This will help you think about how all the attributes of all the inputs get prepared before you do anything with them.\\n\\nThe multi-input POPs that this applies to are:\\n\\nAttribute Combine POP\\nMath Combine POP\\n\\nThe first stage (the preparation stage) is limited:  For all the inputs, and for all the attributes of those inputs you want to:\\n\\n- choose which attributes to pass through\\n- modify\\n- combine with some other attributes\\n\\nLook at Attribute Combine first.  its Inputs page.  There are three blocks of sequential parameters, one for each input.  For each you select some attributes and decide if you want to rename them. In this case you get the position P fromthe third, the color from the second input, and the Text attribute from the first. No other thing is done, they get passed to the output.\\n\\nMath Combine POP deals with the input the same way, but the P attribute of the third input is given a name Protated, to distinguish it from the P attribute of the first input.  The result is passed to the second stage where attributes are combined. Here P and Protated are averaged.\\n\\nThe last Math Combine POP does more. We can wire inputs in, but equally in Attribute Combine or Math Combine, you can give references (path of a node in the In POP par) for any input.  Math Combine does the a similar averaging, then inverts the color, as it can do multiple combines, etc.\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n'"

Titletext.67108928: "Math Combine is a multi-Input POP like Attribute Combine POP where you gather the attributes you want to work with"
